footnotes.text: Which neither . . . to bear?: St. Peter refers to the Law as interpreted by the rabbis who had added countless restrictions.
Idolatry and immorality were so widespread among the pagans that a special warning was felt necessary for the new converts.
The Jews of the Dispersion, well instructed in the Mosaic Law, would find it hard to associate with those who did not observe it strictly on these points at least.
